async_execution.mechanism: Amp hooks run synchronously; no async execution documented

context_injection.mechanism: Amp hooks cannot inject context into the agent session

decision_control.mechanism: Amp hooks are observational; no mechanism to block, allow, or modify tool invocations documented

handler_types.mechanism: Amp hooks execute shell commands only; no other handler types documented

hook_scopes.mechanism: Amp hooks are project-scoped; no multi-scope configuration documented

input_modification.mechanism: Amp hooks cannot modify tool input before execution

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Amp hooks communicate via exit codes; no JSON stdin/stdout protocol documented

matcher_patterns.mechanism: hook_match_input_contains: Amp hooks can match on input content containing specific strings

permission_control.mechanism: permissions_system: Amp has a permissions system that hooks interact with to make tool availability decisions

providerExtensions.name: Hook trigger events
providerExtensions.summary: Two events: 'tool:pre-execute' (before tool runs) and 'tool:post-execute' (after); each event accepts only specific actions.

providerExtensions.name: Hook action: send-user-message
providerExtensions.summary: Pre-execute action that injects a user message and cancels the pending tool call to enforce behavioral constraints.

providerExtensions.name: Hook action: redact-tool-input
providerExtensions.summary: Post-execute action that redacts tool input fields after execution to avoid storing sensitive arguments.

providerExtensions.name: Hook match condition: input.contains
providerExtensions.summary: Filter hook firing by exact literal substring match in tool input — no regex or glob support.

providerExtensions.name: Hook configuration in settings file
providerExtensions.summary: Hook definitions live as an array in Amp settings files (.amp/settings.json or user-scope settings).

providerExtensions.name: Permissions system (amp.permissions)
providerExtensions.summary: Ordered first-match-wins rule system under 'amp.permissions' with allow/reject/ask/delegate actions and per-context defaults.

providerExtensions.name: Permission match condition types
providerExtensions.summary: Match values support globs, regex (slash-delimited), arrays (OR), literals, and nested objects with dot notation.

providerExtensions.name: Permission delegate action
providerExtensions.summary: Delegate decisions to an external program (JSON via stdin, exit code = decision); plugin API is the recommended successor.

providerExtensions.name: Permission context restriction (thread vs subagent)
providerExtensions.summary: Optionally scopes a rule to main thread, sub-agent only, or both (omitted); defaults differ by context.

providerExtensions.name: Permissions CLI (amp permissions)
providerExtensions.summary: 'amp permissions' subcommands (list/add/edit/test) manage rules in a UNIX-shell-style text format without editing JSON.
